customer_name: Levi D. Miller Daniel L. Miller
site_name: Levi D. Miller Daniel L. Miller

citations.narrative:
The facility is not maintaining adequate records of acquisition. The facility utilizes the 7005 forms as the acquisition
records for litters of puppies born on premises. At the time of inspection there was a litter of 7 Boxer puppies (DOB
4/11/2026), a litter of 4 Siberian Husky puppies (DOB 4/9/2026), and a litter of 5 Rottweiler puppies (DOB 4/23/2026)
without an acquisition record. Additionally, an adult Rottweiler acquired approximately 2 weeks ago does not have a
record of acquisition.
Accurate record keeping is essential so that APHIS inspectors can adequately identify the movement of dogs into and out
of the facility. Each dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records for forms which fully and correctly disclose the
information concerning each dog purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, or which is transported, sold, or
otherwise disposed of by that dealer. The records shall include any offspring born to any animal while in his or her control.
Correct by maintaining complete records for acquisition by May 22, 2026.

The surfaces of the shelters in the outdoor housing facilities are not being cleaned and sanitized in an appropriate
manner. Located along the bottom of two primary enclosure walls and the border of the entry/exit doors was the
accumulation of dark brown organic debris and dirt. This area of dirt and grime on the walls and doors extended upward
approximately halfway up the walls of the shelter building. The two primary enclosures were currently housing 2 adult
dogs and 9 puppies. The facility representative could not provide the date of when the surfaces of the shelters were last
cleaned and sanitized.
The accumulation of dirt and organic debris shows the lack of proper cleaning and sanitization. Improper cleaning and
sanitization can increase the risk of contamination and spread of disease hazards to the dogs.
The licensee must ensure that hard surfaces with which the dogs come in contact are spot-cleaned daily and sanitized in
accordance with 3.11(b) to prevent the accumulation of excreta and reduce disease hazards.
Correct by May 22, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.75(a)(1) 
   
Records: Dealers and exhibitors. 
The facility is not maintaining adequate records of acquisition. The facility utilizes the 7005 forms as the acquisition 
records for litters of puppies born on premises. At the time of inspection there was a litter of 7 Boxer puppies (DOB 
4/11/2026), a litter of 4 Siberian Husky puppies (DOB 4/9/2026), and a litter of 5 Rottweiler puppies (DOB 4/23/2026) 
without an acquisition record. Additionally, an adult Rottweiler acquired approximately 2 weeks ago does not have a 
record of acquisition.  
 
Accurate record keeping is essential so that APHIS inspectors can adequately identify the movement of dogs into and out 
of the facility. Each dealer shall make, keep, and maintain records for forms which fully and correctly disclose the 
information concerning each dog purchased or otherwise acquired, owned, held, or which is transported, sold, or 
otherwise disposed of by that dealer. The records shall include any offspring born to any animal while in his or her control.  
Correct by maintaining complete records for acquisition by May 22, 2026. 
3.1(c)(3) 
   
Housing facilities, general. 
The surfaces of the shelters in the outdoor housing facilities are not being cleaned and sanitized in an appropriate 
manner. Located along the bottom of two primary enclosure walls and the border of the entry/exit doors was the 
accumulation of dark brown organic debris and dirt. This area of dirt and grime on the walls and doors extended upward 
approximately halfway up the walls of the shelter building. The two primary enclosures were currently housing 2 adult 
dogs and 9 puppies. The facility representative could not provide the date of when the surfaces of the shelters were last 
cleaned and sanitized.  
 
The accumulation of dirt and organic debris shows the lack of proper cleaning and sanitization. Improper cleaning and 
sanitization can increase the risk of contamination and spread of disease hazards to the dogs. 
 
The licensee must ensure that hard surfaces with which the dogs come in contact are spot-cleaned daily and sanitized in 
accordance with 3.11(b) to prevent the accumulation of excreta and reduce disease hazards.  
Correct by May 22, 2026

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
